course.title: Lesson 48: Did You Want to Tell Me Something
course.audioFilename: 48－Did You Want to Tell Me Something.mp3

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why did the writer become very worried?

translations.zh-Hans: 作者为什么变得非常担心？

segments.text: Dentists always ask questions when it is impossible for you to answer.

translations.zh-Hans: 牙医总是在你无法回答时问问题。

segments.text: My dentist had just pulled out one of my teeth and had told me to rest for a while.

translations.zh-Hans: 我的牙医刚拔掉我的一颗牙，并让我休息一会儿。

segments.text: I tried to say something, but my mouth was full of cotton wool.

translations.zh-Hans: 我想说点什么，可嘴里塞满了药棉。

segments.text: He knew I collected match boxes and asked me whether my collection was growing.

translations.zh-Hans: 他知道我收集火柴盒，便问我的收藏是否在增加。

segments.text: He then asked me how my brother was and whether I liked my new job in London.

translations.zh-Hans: 接着，他又问我哥哥身体如何，以及我是否喜欢在伦敦的新工作。

segments.text: In answer to these questions, I either nodded or made strange noises.

translations.zh-Hans: 回答这些问题时，我不是点头，就是发出奇怪的声音。

segments.text: Meanwhile, my tongue was busy searching out the hole where the tooth had been.

translations.zh-Hans: 与此同时，我的舌头正忙着寻找原来牙齿所在的那个洞。

segments.text: I suddenly felt very worried, but could not say anything.

translations.zh-Hans: 我突然非常担心，却什么也说不出来。

segments.text: When the dentist at last removed the cotton wool from my mouth, I was able to tell him that he had pulled out the wrong tooth.

translations.zh-Hans: 牙医终于从我嘴里取出药棉后，我才得以告诉他，他拔错了牙。
